behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure usually appears at module handoff boundaries (manifest parser + lockfile/graph resolver + scanner input assembly). A unit test of one parser function can pass while transitive deps are dropped in wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner dependency collection against a fixture project containing direct deps plus nested transitive deps (via lockfile). Assert the final vulnerability-check input includes the full expected set (name+resolved version) for both direct and transitive nodes.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real API semantics for version-range matching. In-process mocks can trivially return expected CVEs and hide real request-shape/filtering mismatches.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real (or production-like) vulnerability API endpoint, submit a manifest fixture with the same package at multiple version ranges where CVE applicability differs by range. Assert returned CVEs match the exact range and exclude non-applicable versions.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic ordering logic within one function/object boundary; no I/O or external infra is required to expose ranking mistakes.
behaviors.test_description: Feed an unsorted CVE list covering all severities and mixed CVSS scores, including ties and missing-score edge cases. Assert exact ordered output: severity precedence first, then descending CVSS within severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failures often come from parse/modify/serialize pipeline behavior (reformatting, key reordering, accidental edits) rather than single-function logic. A pure unit test can miss file-level mutations.
behaviors.test_description: Apply the patch routine to real manifest fixtures (JSON and text formats where supported) on disk. Diff before/after and assert only the targeted dependency version token changed; all other lines/fields remain byte-for-byte unchanged where format guarantees allow.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real git hosting API/network behavior and repository state transitions (branch, commit, PR creation). Local mocks frequently miss auth, permission, branch protection, and diff-upload failures.
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repository, run scanner auto-fix flow for one critical vuln. Assert a PR is actually opened, references the expected branch/commit, and the PR diff contains exactly one dependency version change and no unrelated files.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Semver major-bump detection is deterministic local logic and can be validated at function boundary with exhaustive edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Table-drive semver pairs (including pre-release/build metadata and non-standard prefixes) and assert breaking-change flag is true only when effective major version increases.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module control-flow behavior: semver decision -> PR orchestrator gating -> reporting output. Unit tests on any single module can miss incorrect handoff/state propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner end-to-end locally with a fixture requiring a major bump. Use a spy/fake PR client in the integrated pipeline and assert no PR creation call occurs, while scanner output includes the explicit review-required flag/reason.
